mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient real output despite higher rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 50% of observations during tightening periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, persistent inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of historical cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the GDP growth signal remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in real economic activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
